title: westringia cheelii
intro: Westringia cheelii, also known as the Australian frost flower or coastal rosemary, is a flowering shrub native to eastern Australia. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Westringia cheelii have been used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Westringia cheelii can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
